<section class="detail" id="getPool()">
<h3>getPool</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getPool</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).pool().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A Pool is the graphical representation of a Participant in a Collaboration. A Participant can be a specific PartnerEntity (e.g., a company) or can be a more general PartnerRole (e.g., a buyer, seller, or manufacturer). A Pool MAY or MAY NOT reference a Process. A Pool is NOT REQUIRED to contain a Process, i.e., it can be a    black box.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isPool(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).processDiagram().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">ProcessDiagram is a concrete DiagramDefinition that defines diagrams that reference a BPMN Process element as a context.
         Diagram may own lanes.
         Processes (Orchestration), includes:
         o Private Non-executable (internal) Business Processes
         o Private Executable (internal) Business Processes
         o Public Processes</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>

<section class="detail" id="getReceiveTask()">
<h3>getReceiveTask</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getReceiveTask</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).receiveTask().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A Receive Task is a simple Task that is designed to wait for a Message to arrive from an external Participant (relative to the Process). Once the Message has been received, the Task is completed.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

 <a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a> element)</span></div>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getRendering()">
<h3>getRendering</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getRendering</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).rendering().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">BPMN User Tasks need to be rendered on user interfaces like forms clients, portlets, etc. The Rendering element provides an extensible mechanism for specifying UI renderings for User Tasks (Task UI). The element is optional. One or more rendering methods may be provided in a Task definition. A User Task can be deployed on any compliant implementation, irrespective of the fact whether the implementation supports specified rendering methods or not. The Rendering element is the extension point for renderings. Things like language considerations are opaque for the Rendering element because the rendering applications typically provide MultiLanguage support. Where this is not the case, providers of certain rendering types may decide to extend the rendering type in order to provide language information for a given rendering. The content of the rendering element is not defined by this specification.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isRendering(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">

</span><span class="modifiers">public static</span> <span class="return-type">boolean</span> <span class="element-name">isRendering</span><wbr/><span class="parameters">(@CheckForNull
 <a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a> element)</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use RenderingStereotype.isInstance(element)</div>
</div>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="getResource()">
<h3>getResource</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getResource</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).resource().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">The Resource class is used to specify resources that can be referenced by Activities. These Resources can be Human Resources as well as any other resource assigned to Activities during Process execution time.
         The definition of a Resource is "abstract", because it only defines the Resource, without detailing how e.g. actual user IDs are associated at runtime. Multiple Activities can utilize the same Resource.
         Every Resource can define a set of ResourceParameters. These parameters can be used at runtime to define query e.g. into an Organizational Directory. Every Activity referencing a parameterized Resource can bind values available in the scope of the Activity to these parameters.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

 <a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a> element)</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>

</div>
</section>
</li>

<section class="detail" id="getResourceParameter()">
<h3>getResourceParameter</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getResourceParameter</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).resourceParameter().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">Resource can define a set of parameters to define a query to resolve the actual resources (e.g. user ids). The ResourceParameter element inherits the attributes and model associations of BaseElement through its relationship to RootElement, and adds additional model associations:
         type: Element - specifies the type of the query parameter
         isRequired: Boolean - specifies, if a parameter is optional or mandatory.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getRootElement()">
<h3>getRootElement</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getRootElement</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).rootElement().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">RootElement is the abstract super class for all BPMN elements that are contained within Definitions. When contained within Definitions, these elements have their own defined life-cycle and are not deleted with the deletion of other elements. Examples of concrete RootElements include Collaboration, Process, and Choreography. Depending on their use, RootElements can be referenced by multiple other elements (i.e., they can be reused). Some RootElements may be contained within other elements instead of Definitions. This is done to avoid the maintenance overhead of an independent life-cycle. For example, an EventDefinition may be contained in a Process since it may be only required there. In this case the EventDefinition would be dependent on the tool life-cycle of the Process.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<section class="detail" id="getScriptTask()">
<h3>getScriptTask</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getScriptTask</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).scriptTask().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A Script Task is executed by a business process engine. The modeler or implementer defines a script in a language that the engine can interpret. When the Task is ready to start, the engine will execute the script. When the script is completed, the Task will also be completed.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>

<section class="detail" id="getSequenceFlow()">
<h3>getSequenceFlow</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getSequenceFlow</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).sequenceFlow().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A Sequence Flow is used to show the order of Flow Elements in a Process. Each Sequence Flow has only one source and only one target. The source and target must be from the set of the following Flow Elements: Events (Start, Intermediate, and End), Activities (Task and Sub-Process), and Gateways.
         A Sequence Flow can optionally define a condition expression, indicating that the    transfer of control    will only be available if the expression evaluates to true. This expression is typically used when the source of the Sequence Flow is a Gateway or an Activity.
         A Sequence Flow that has an Exclusive, Inclusive, or Complex Gateway or an Activity as its source can also be defined with as default. Such Sequence Flow will have a marker to show that it is a default flow. The default Sequence Flow is taken (a token is passed) only if all the other outgoing Sequence Flow from the Activity or Gateway are not valid (i.e., their condition Expressions are false).</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<section class="detail" id="getSignalBoundaryEvent()">
<h3>getSignalBoundaryEvent</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getSignalBoundaryEvent</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).signalBoundaryEvent().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">The Signal Event can only receive a Signal when attached to the boundary of an activity. In this context, it will change the Normal Flow into an Exception Flow upon being triggered. The Signal Event differs from an Error Event in that the Signal defines a more general, non-error condition for interrupting activities (such as the successful completion of another activity) as well as having a larger scope than Error Events. When used to    catch    the signal, the Event marker will be unfilled.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isSignalBoundaryEvent(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">
<h3>isSignalBoundaryEvent</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getSignalCatchIntermediateEvent()">
<h3>getSignalCatchIntermediateEvent</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getSignalCatchIntermediateEvent</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).signalCatchIntermediateEvent().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">This type of event is used for sending or receiving Signals. A Signal is for general communication within and across Process Levels, across Pools, and between Business Process Diagrams. A BPMN Signal is similar to a signal flare that shot into the sky for anyone who might be interested to notice and then react. Thus, there is a source of the Signal, but no specific intended target. This type of Intermediate Event can send or receive a Signal if the Event is part of a Normal Flow. The Event can only receive a Signal when attached to the boundary of an activity. The Signal Event differs from an Error Event in that the Signal defines a more general, non-error condition for interrupting activities (such as the successful completion of another activity) as well as having a larger scope than Error Events.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getStandardLoopCharacteristics()">
<h3>getStandardLoopCharacteristics</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getStandardLoopCharacteristics</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).standardLoopCharacteristics().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">The StandardLoopCharacteristics class defines looping behavior based on a boolean condition.
         The Activity will loop as long as the boolean condition is true. The condition is evaluated for every loop iteration, and may be evaluated at the beginning or at the end of the iteration. In addition, a numeric cap can be optionally specified. The number of iterations may not exceed this cap.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isStandardLoopCharacteristics(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getStartEvent()">
<h3>getStartEvent</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getStartEvent</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).startEvent().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">As the name implies, the Start Event indicates where a particular Process will start. In terms of Sequence Flow, the Start Event starts the flow of the Process, and thus, will not have any incoming Sequence Flow     no Sequence Flow can connect to a Start Event.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<li>
<section class="detail" id="getSubChoreography()">
<h3>getSubChoreography</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getSubChoreography</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).subChoreography().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A Choreography Sub-Process is a compound Activity in that it has detail that is defined as a flow of other Activities, in this case, a Choreography. Each Choreography Sub-Process involves two (2) or more Participants. The name of the Choreography Sub-Process and each of the Participants are all displayed in
         the different bands that make up the shape   s graphical notation. There are two (2) more Participant Bands and one Sub-Process Name Band.
         The Choreography Sub-Process can be in a collapsed view that hides its details, or a Choreography Sub-Process can be expanded to show its details (a Choreography Process) within the Choreography Process in which it is contained (see Figure 12-19). In the collapsed form, the Sub-Process object uses a marker to distinguish it as a Choreography Sub-Process, rather than a Choreography Task.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isSubChoreography(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">
<h3>isSubChoreography</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public static</span> <span class="return-type">boolean</span> <span class="element-name">isSubChoreography</span><wbr/><span class="parameters">(@CheckForNull
 <a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a> element)</span></div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>

</div>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="getSubConversation()">
<h3>getSubConversation</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getSubConversation</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).subConversation().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block"><html>
<head>
</head>
<body>
<p>
         A Sub-Conversation is a ConversationNode that is a hierarchical division
         within the parent Conversation. A Sub-Conversation is a graphical object
         within a Conversation, but it also can be “opened up” to show the
         lower-level Conversation, which consist of Message Flow, Communications,
         and/or other Sub-Conversations. The Sub-Conversation shares the
         Participants of its parent Conversation.
         </p>
</body>
</html></div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isSubConversation(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">
<h3>isSubConversation</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public static</span> <span class="return-type">boolean</span> <span class="element-name">isSubConversation</span><wbr/><span class="parameters">(@CheckForNull
 <a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a> element)</span></div>

</div>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="getSubProcess()">
<h3>getSubProcess</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getSubProcess</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).subProcess().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A Sub-Process is an Activity whose internal details have been modeled using Activities, Gateways, Events, and Sequence Flow. A Sub-Process is a graphical object within a Process, but it also can be    opened up    to show a lower-level Process. Sub-Processes define a contextual scope that can be used for attribute visibility, transactional scope, for the handling of exceptions, of Events, or for compensation.
         There are different types of Sub-Processes.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="isSubProcess(com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element)">

 <a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/classes/mdkernel/Element.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel">Element</a> element)</span></div>

</div>
</section>
</li>
<li>
<section class="detail" id="getTask()">
<h3>getTask</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getTask</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).task().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A Task is an atomic Activity within a Process flow. A Task is used when the work in the Process cannot be broken down to a finer level of detail. Generally, an end-user and/or applications are used to perform the Task when it is executed.
         There are different types of Tasks identified within BPMN to separate the types of inherent behavior that Tasks might represent. The list of Task types may be extended along with any corresponding indicators. A Task which is not further specified is called Abstract Task.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>

</div>
</section>

<section class="detail" id="getThrowEvent()">
<h3>getThrowEvent</h3>
<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>
</span><span class="modifiers">public</span> <span class="return-type"><a href="../../../uml2/ext/magicdraw/mdprofiles/Stereotype.html" title="interface in com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles">Stereotype</a></span> <span class="element-name">getThrowEvent</span>()</div>
<div class="deprecation-block"><span class="deprecated-label">Deprecated.</span>
<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).throwEvent().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">Events that throw a Result. All End Events and some Intermediate Events are throwing Events that may eventually be caught by another Event. Typically the Trigger carries information out of the scope where the throw Event occurred into the scope of the catching Events. The throwing of a trigger may be either implicit as defined by this standard or an extension to it or explicit by a throw Event.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
<li>

<div class="member-signature"><span class="annotations"><a class="external-link" href="https://docs.oracle.com/en/java/javase/17/docs/api/java.base/java/lang/Deprecated.html" title="class or interface in java.lang">@Deprecated</a>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).timerBoundaryEvent().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">A specific time-date or a specific cycle (e.g., every Monday at 9 am) can be set that will trigger the Event. If a Timer Event is attached to the boundary of an Activity, it will change the Normal Flow into an Exception Flow upon
         being triggered.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>

<div class="deprecation-comment">use getInstance(element).timerCatchIntermediateEvent().getStereotype()</div>
</div>
<div class="block">In Normal Flow the Timer Intermediate Event acts as a delay mechanism based on a specific time-date or a specific cycle (e.g., every Monday at 9 am) can be set that will trigger the Event.</div>
<dl class="notes">
<dt>Returns:</dt>
<dd>stereotype</dd>
</dl>
</section>
</li>
